source_jobs.title: Solutions Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FORMAL

Formal is data security platform designed to help teams understand and control their data on autopilot.

We're building a modern protocol-aware reverse-proxy for datastores and APIs that helps organizations understand their data in real-time. This allows teams to see how sensitive data is stored, consumed, and used in order to enforce least privilege through masking, filtering, or anonymization policies.

We are trusted by leading companies such as Ramp http://Ramp.com, Gusto http://Gusto.com, Notion http://Notion.com to solve problems across data security and compliance, data quality management, and infrastructure access.

Formal is backed by top-tiers VCs including Thrive Capital and Y Combinator with angel investors that include executives and founders from Datadog, Clickhouse, Plaid, and Vanta.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Formal is on a mission to build the best engineering team and we're looking for people who want to make outsized impact on shaping the product and building the company.

We're looking for a Solutions Engineer who will partner closely with our customers and prospects to deeply understand their infrastructure and workflows, guide technical evaluations, and ensure successful deployments and ongoing value.

This is a hybrid role that blends strong technical acumen with customer-facing empathy. You’ll collaborate with engineering, product, and GTM teams to solve complex customer problems and influence the roadmap based on real-world needs.

If you're someone who loves diving into technical architecture, explaining complex topics clearly, and helping teams adopt powerful products, this role is for you.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Act as the technical point of contact for prospects and customers during onboarding, pilots, and expansions

 - Lead deep technical conversations with engineering, security, and data teams at modern companies

 - Design and deliver customized solution architectures that demonstrate the value of Formal's platform

 - Work closely with engineering to prioritize and scope technical requests or integrations

 - Build internal tooling, scripts, or resources to accelerate onboarding and repeatability

 - Develop and maintain deep expertise in Formal’s product, data protocols, and adjacent ecosystem (e.g., Postgres, Kafka, S3, Snowflake)

 - Gather feedback and relay customer insights to product and engineering to help shape the roadmad
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - Experience in a solutions engineering, sales engineering, or implementation engineering role at a developer-facing company

 - Strong technical background — you’ve written production code, debugged systems, or run infrastructure before

 - Ability to clearly explain complex concepts to both technical and non-technical audiences

 - Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and modern infrastructure tools

 - Comfort working across a variety of systems, APIs, and data protocols (e.g., Postgres, Kafka, MySQL, Snowflake, S3)

 - A collaborative mindset and a high sense of ownership — you're proactive and love solving problems hands-on

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills
   
   


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - You have worked in a fast, high-growth startup environment before

 - You have experience using tools like Temporal and Render

 - You have extensive knowledge of datastore protocols (Postgres, S3, Kafka)
   
   


COMPENSATION

 - This role offers cash compensation and a stock options grant.

 - The positioning of offers within a certain range depends on various factors, including: candidate experience, qualifications, skills, and business requirements.
   
   


BENEFITS (FOR U.S.-BASED FULL-TIME EMPLOYEES)

 - 100% medical, dental & vision insurance coverage for you
   
   - Partially covered for your dependents

 - Flexible PTO

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer | Backend
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FORMAL

Formal is data security platform designed to help teams understand and control their data on autopilot.

We're building a modern protocol-aware reverse-proxy for datastores and APIs that helps organizations understand their data in real-time. This allows teams to see how sensitive data is stored, consumed, and used in order to enforce least privilege through masking, filtering, or anonymization policies.

We are trusted by leading companies such as Ramp http://Ramp.com, Gusto http://Gusto.com, Notion http://Notion.com to solve problems across data security and compliance, data quality management, and infrastructure access.

Formal is backed by top-tiers VCs including Thrive Capital and Y Combinator with angel investors that include executives and founders from Datadog, Clickhouse, Plaid, and Vanta.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Formal is on a mission to build the best engineering team and we're looking for people who want to make outsized impact on shaping the product and building the company.

Our ideal candidate has a deep understanding of building scalable backend systems, a strong sense of ownership, and enjoys owning projects from inception to scaling it in production.

Our tech stack currently involves Golang, Rust, Temporal, Datastore protocols (Postgres, S3, Kafka, MySQL, Snowflake, Redshift, SSH), and AWS




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and develop a high performance data platform that can handle large volumes of data in real-time

 - Build new product features from start to finish through to research, implementation, and maintenance

 - Help improve performance and reliability of net new and existing features

 - Lead any initiative you think may have a strong impact on Formal

 - Design scalable services as Formal grows and processes more traffic
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - Software development experience building data-intensive software platforms

 - You value code simplicity and performance

 - Strong knowledge of Golang and experience with its ecosystem

 - Experience with streaming data processing frameworks such as Apache Kafka and Google Pub/Sub

 - Experience debugging and optimizing complex production systems

 - Experience working in DBs, OS, networking, or other systems-level environments

 - Experience with containerization technologies (Docker, ECS, or Kubernetes) and infrastructure as a code technologies (Terraform)

 - Excellent communication skills and the ability to work well within a team

 - You are a strong problem solver and have solid production debugging skills
   
   


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - You have worked in a fast, high-growth startup environment before

 - You have experience using tools like Temporal and Render

 - You have extensive knowledge of datastore protocols (Postgres, S3, Kafka)

 - Familiarity with cloud computing platforms, such as Azure or GCP
   
   


COMPENSATION

 - This role offers cash compensation and a stock options grant.

 - The positioning of offers within a certain range depends on various factors, including: candidate experience, qualifications, skills, and business requirements.
   
   


BENEFITS (FOR U.S.-BASED FULL-TIME EMPLOYEES)

 - 100% medical, dental & vision insurance coverage for you
   
   - Partially covered for your dependents

 - Flexible PTO

source_jobs.title: Founding Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FORMAL

Formal is data security platform designed to help teams understand and control their data.

We're building a modern protocol-aware reverse-proxy for datastores and APIs that helps organizations understand their data in real-time. This allows teams to see how sensitive data is stored, consumed, and used in order to enforce least privilege through masking, filtering, or anonymization policies.

We are trusted by leading companies such as Ramp http://Ramp.com, Gusto http://Gusto.com, Notion http://Notion.com to solve problems across data security and compliance, data quality management, and infrastructure access.

Formal is backed by top-tiers VCs including Thrive Capital and Y Combinator with angel investors that include executives and founders from Datadog, Clickhouse, Plaid, and Vanta.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Founding Designer you will play a crucial role in shaping and communicating Formal's story by owning UI/UX, visual design, branding, and video production. You’ll have ownership of our design strategy and execution, working directly with the CEO and collaborating closely with engineering.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design Interfaces: Develop intuitive UI/UX designs for our app using Figma while engaging directly with customers to shape product design.

 - Design and maintain our website. Create visually striking and intuitive web experiences that showcase our product and brand narrative.

 - Craft Visuals: Create engaging visuals and dynamic content for marketing, launches, and case studies.

 - Drive Branding: Ensure design consistency across Formal's brand identity.

 - Collaborate Cross-functionally: Work directly with the team to align design strategies with business goals.

 - Innovate: Stay ahead of design trends, ensuring Formal remains visually compelling and competitive.
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - Experience in product or brand design, preferably in B2B SaaS or cybersecurity industries

 - Standout portfolio showcasing UI/UX designs, marketing assets, and brand design

 - Ability to fully own projects from concept to delivery without oversight

 - Comfortable in fast-paced environments with rapid iteration cycles

 - Strong proficiency in Figma for UI/UX, prototyping, and graphic design

 - Skilled in marketing design (social media posts, banners, promotional materials)

 - Solid foundation in design principles, typography, and color theory

 - Quick learner, eager to dive into new ideas and tools
   
   


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience working in or with early-stage startups in the security or data management space

 - Strong project management skills with the ability to manage multiple initiatives simultaneously

 - Creativity and innovation in developing marketing strategies and content

 - Experience in video editing (Adobe Premiere Pro, After Effects, or Final Cut Pro) is a plus
   
   


COMPENSATION

 - This role offers cash compensation and a stock options grant.

 - The positioning of offers within a certain range depends on various factors, including: candidate experience, qualifications, skills, business requirements, and geographical location.
   
   


BENEFITS (FOR U.S.-BASED FULL-TIME EMPLOYEES)

 - 100% medical, dental & vision insurance coverage for you
   
   - Partially covered for your dependents

 - Flexible PTO

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer | Fullstack
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FORMAL

Formal is data security platform designed to help teams understand and control their data on autopilot.

We're building a modern protocol-aware reverse-proxy for datastores and APIs that helps organizations understand their data in real-time. This allows teams to see how sensitive data is stored, consumed, and used in order to enforce least privilege through masking, filtering, or anonymization policies.

We are trusted by leading companies such as Ramp http://Ramp.com, Gusto http://Gusto.com, Notion http://Notion.com to solve problems across data security and compliance, data quality management, and infrastructure access.

Formal is backed by top-tiers VCs including Thrive Capital and Y Combinator with angel investors that include executives and founders from Datadog, Clickhouse, Plaid, and Vanta.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Formal is on a mission to build the best engineering team and we're looking for people who want to make outsized impact on shaping the product and building the company.

Our ideal candidate has a deep understanding of building scalable systems, a strong sense of ownership, and enjoys owning projects from inception to scaling it in production.

Our tech stack currently involves Typescript, NextJS, Shadcn, Vercel, and Golang




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Continuously improve the performance and reliability of our frontend application by optimizing code and identifying areas for enhancement.

 - Enhance existing features by incorporating user feedback and implementing improvements to usability and design.

 - Take ownership of the frontend architecture, ensuring scalability, maintainability, and adherence to best practices.

 - Lead any initiative you think may have a strong impact on Formal.
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - Relevant software development experience building data-intensive software platforms

 - Strong expertise in a modern framework (e.g. React, Vue.js) and proficiency with some backend language (we use Go)

 - Experience with streaming data processing frameworks such as Apache Kafka and Google Pub/Sub

 - Some experience with underlying infrastructure primitives like Kubernetes and Terraform

 - Experience in building scalable, responsive, and cross-browser compatible web applications

 - Solid understanding of frontend development principles, including testing, performance optimization, code modularity, and accessibility

 - Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to tackle complex technical challenges with innovative solutions

 - Excellent collaboration and communication skills, with the ability to work effectively in cross-functional teams
   
   


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Early engineer at a hyper-growth startup.

 - Extensive experience designing applications on Figma

 - Experience contributing to a design framework

 - Experience with UI/UX and product design
   
   


COMPENSATION

 - This role offers cash compensation and stock options.

 - The positioning of offers within a certain range depends on various factors, including: candidate experience, qualifications, skills, business requirements, and geographical location.
   
   


BENEFITS (FOR U.S.-BASED FULL-TIME EMPLOYEES)

 - 100% medical, dental & vision insurance coverage for you
   
   - Partially covered for your dependents

 - Flexible PTO

source_jobs.title: Field Marketing Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FORMAL

Formal is building the future of privilege access management - and we throw a great party while we’re at it. From founder dinners in SF to conferences like RSA, our events are where we meet the security leaders who become our customers. We’re hiring a Field Marketer to own that experience end-to-end.



ABOUT THE ROLE

A Field Marketing Specialist is a highly organized person who can do the hands-on work of managing logistics and online and offline details for projects taking place in person. This includes intimate dinners and lunches, meetups in our SF office, and large-scale conference programs (RSA, Black Hat, and the satellite events we host around them). This is a hands-on, high-ownership role.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the field marketing calendar - dinners, lunches, in-office meetups, conferences and satellite events

 - Run the full event lifecycle: venue and vendor sourcing, contracts, invites, registration, on-site execution, run pre- and post-event campaign motions to maximize attendance, engagement, follow-up, and conversion

 - Partner closely with sales and exec leadership to build guest lists, create targets and pipeline goals, and tell a story that lands with security and engineering buyers.

 - Track what works: attendance, pipeline influence and the qualitative signal that makes an event memorable.
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - Experience: 3-5 years of field marketing experience, preferably in B2B tech or cybersecurity, with demonstrated expertise in partner marketing strategies.

 - Communication Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a strong ability to collaborate across functions and build relationships with stakeholders.

 - Event Expertise: Track record running large conference programs (RSA, Black Hat, Re:Invent, KubeCon, or similar) as a vendor - not just attending.

 - Sharp eye for detail - menu, signage, lighting, name tags, the vibe

 - Occasional domestic travel to support events
   


WHY FORMAL http://Formal.ai

 - Real ownership - you’ll shape how we show up to the security community.

 - A team that takes events seriously as a growth channel and gives you the budget to do them right.

 - Beautiful SF office that doubles as a venue, plus the team and budget to back you up.

 - Competitive comp, equity, full benefits.

 - 100% medical, dental & vision insurance coverage for you
   
   - Partially covered for your dependents

 - Flexible PTO

source_jobs.title: Channel Account Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FORMAL

Formal is data security platform designed to help teams understand and control their data on autopilot.

We're building a modern protocol-aware reverse-proxy for datastores and APIs that helps organizations understand their data in real-time. This allows teams to see how sensitive data is stored, consumed, and used in order to enforce least privilege through masking, filtering, or anonymization policies.

We are trusted by leading companies such as Ramp http://Ramp.com, Gusto http://Gusto.com, Notion http://Notion.com to solve problems across data security and compliance, data quality management, and infrastructure access.

Formal is backed by top-tiers VCs including Thrive Capital and Y Combinator with angel investors that include executives and founders from Datadog, Clickhouse, Plaid, and Vanta.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Channel Account Specialist, you will play a crucial role in shaping Formal's relationships with channel partners. You'll work closely with our product, sales, and leadership teams to develop and execute partner strategies that drive growth and establish Formal as a leader in the data security space.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Strategic Planning: Develop and implement a comprehensive channel strategy to drive revenue growth and expand the partner ecosystem.

 - Partner Recruitment: Identify, recruit, and onboard new channel partners, including resellers, distributors, and strategic alliances.

 - Relationship Management: Build and maintain strong, long-term relationships with key partners, serving as the primary point of contact and advocate for their needs.

 - Sales Enablement: Provide partners with the necessary tools, training, and resources to effectively sell and support our data security solutions.

 - Performance Management: Set performance metrics, monitor partner performance, and implement improvement plans as needed to ensure partners meet and exceed sales targets.

 - Marketing Collaboration: Work closely with the marketing team to develop and execute joint marketing campaigns and events that drive demand and generate leads through the channel.

 - Forecasting and Reporting: Provide accurate sales forecasts and regular performance reports to senior management, highlighting successes, challenges, and opportunities for growth.

 - Product Knowledge: Stay up-to-date with our product offerings and industry trends to effectively communicate value propositions to partners and end customers.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate with internal teams, including sales, marketing, product management, and customer support, to ensure alignment and success of channel initiatives.
   
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - Minimum of 5 years of experience in channel sales or partner management, preferably in the data security or technology industry.

 - Demonstrated success in developing and managing a high-performing channel partner network within the security industry with a track record of achieving and exceeding sales targets.

 - Exceptional interpersonal and relationship-building skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate effectively with partners at all levels.

 - Strong strategic and analytical skills, with the ability to develop and execute effective channel strategies.

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present and articulate complex concepts clearly and persuasively.

 - Strong leadership and team management skills, with the ability to inspire and motivate both internal teams and external partners.

 - Ability to understand and communicate technical concepts related to data security solutions.

 - Willingness to travel as needed to meet with partners and attend industry events.



COMPENSATION

 - This role offers cash compensation and a stock options grant.

 - The positioning of offers within a certain range depends on various factors, including: candidate experience, qualifications, skills, business requirements and geographical locatio

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FORMAL

Formal is data security platform designed to help teams understand and control their data on autopilot.

We're building a modern protocol-aware reverse-proxy for datastores and APIs that helps organizations understand their data in real-time. This allows teams to see how sensitive data is stored, consumed, and used in order to enforce least privilege through masking, filtering, or anonymization policies.

We are trusted by leading companies such as Ramp http://Ramp.com, Gusto http://Gusto.com, Notion http://Notion.com to solve problems across data security and compliance, data quality management, and infrastructure access.

Formal is backed by top-tiers VCs including Thrive Capital and Y Combinator with angel investors that include executives and founders from Datadog, Clickhouse, Plaid, and Vanta.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for account executives who want to roll up their sleeves and help close deals and develop our go-to-market motion. You’ll need to iterate on positioning, lead generation, and closing processes. We're looking for people to develop our GTM strategy and close key customer accounts as they build the sales process.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Generate leads through cold calling, research, networking, and driving your territory

 - Manage and convert inbound leads to drive revenue growth

 - Develop and implement full sales cycle strategies for driving product adoption, net new product sales, and client retention at scale among existing Formal customers

 - Collaborate with internal teams to ensure customer satisfaction and successful onboarding

 - Track and report on sales performance metrics

 - Gather client feedback to inform product improvements and enhancements

 - Chat with potential customers, understand their business challenges, and explain how Formal can make help them protect their sensitive data
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - 5+ years of experience in a Sales role at an early stage startup (seed to series B) with a technical product

 - Ability to understand complex technical ecosystems and build great relationships with technical customers

 - You have the drive and willingness to go deep on our product to carry a technical conversation with security teams.

 - Analytical ability to manage complex sales cycles

 - Excellent communication skills and the ability to work well within a team
   
   


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience in a fast, high-growth startup environment

 - Experience using tools like Temporal and Render

 - Extensive knowledge of datastore protocols (Postgres, S3, Kafka)

 - Familiarity with cloud computing platforms such as AWS or GCP

 - Fastidious approach to CRM hygiene and experience utilizing thorough sales forecasting metrics on a weekly/quarterly basis and communicating them in a succinct manner
   
   


COMPENSATION

 - This role offers cash compensation and a stock options grant.

 - The positioning of offers within a certain range depends on various factors, including: candidate experience, qualifications, skills, business requirements, and geographical location.
   
   


BENEFITS (FOR U.S.-BASED FULL-TIME EMPLOYEES)

 - 100% medical, dental & vision insurance coverage for you
   
   - Partially covered for your dependents

 - Flexible PTO

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FORMAL

Formal is data security platform designed to help teams understand and control their data on autopilot.

We're building a modern protocol-aware reverse-proxy for datastores and APIs that helps organizations understand their data in real-time. This allows teams to see how sensitive data is stored, consumed, and used in order to enforce least privilege through masking, filtering, or anonymization policies.

We are trusted by leading companies such as Ramp http://Ramp.com, Gusto http://Gusto.com, Notion http://Notion.com to solve problems across data security and compliance, data quality management, and infrastructure access.

Formal is backed by top-tiers VCs including Thrive Capital and Y Combinator with angel investors that include executives and founders from Datadog, Clickhouse, Plaid, and Vanta.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Product Marketing Manager, you will play a crucial role in shaping and communicating Formal's value proposition to our target market. You'll work closely with our product, sales, and leadership teams to develop and execute marketing strategies that drive growth and establish Formal as a leader in the data security space.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Develop and execute marketing strategies that align with Formal's business objectives and target audiences

 - Build marketing campaigns (including in-person events, digital events, social/PR, customer marketing) to drive sales pipeline and acquire new customers

 - Create and maintain product positioning and messaging that differentiates Formal in the market

 - Produce high-quality marketing collateral, including whitepapers, case studies, blog posts, and sales enablement materials

 - Work closely with the sales team to develop and refine pitch decks, demo scripts, and other sales tools

 - Plan and execute product launches, ensuring all teams are aligned and prepared

 - Analyze marketing performance metrics and provide insights on how to optimize ROI, strategies, and tactics.
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED

 - 5+ years of experience in product marketing, preferably in B2B SaaS or cybersecurity industries

 - Proven track record of successfully launching and marketing complex technical products

 - Strong understanding of data security, compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA), and cloud technologies

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts into clear, compelling messages for various audiences

 - Experience working with sales teams to develop effective go-to-market strategies and sales enablement materials

 - Analytical mindset with the ability to use data to inform decision-making and measure marketing impact

 - Collaborative spirit with the ability to work effectively across different teams and stakeholders






NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience working in or with early-stage startups in the security or data management space

 - Strong project management skills with the ability to manage multiple initiatives simultaneously

 - Creativity and innovation in developing marketing strategies and content

 - Passion for technology and staying current with industry trends
   
   


COMPENSATION

 - This role offers cash compensation and a stock options grant.

 - The positioning of offers within a certain range depends on various factors, including: candidate experience, qualifications, skills, business requirements and geographical location.
